scope: Triage of 20 failures remaining after bug-found-001/002/003 fixes shipped

windows-teardown-ebusy.description: Refined diagnosis: NOT discarded handles like bug-002. The test correctly closes its own DB; the leak was WAL/SHM sibling files lingering after openRuntimeDb (which enables WAL mode) in src/commands/context-health.js. Fix: added wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before db.close() in production code + cleanupTmpDir helper in afterEach as a safety net.
windows-teardown-ebusy.resolution: context-health 7/7 green. Full suite delta: 2407/2422 (was 2402/2422). 6 EBUSY recoveries, 1 flake offset elsewhere.
files: tests/context-health.test.js:77:flags heavy and critical files by token count
tests/context-health.test.js:125:sorts files by token count descending

real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.description: ROOT CAUSE: live session keys carry the format `direct-session:{ts}:{agent}` with colons. NTFS reserves `:` in filenames — `mkdir 'direct-session:123:deyvin'` fails with ENOENT on Windows. The fs.mkdir calls inside writeLiveState/appendLiveEvent/writeLiveSummary were wrapped in `try { ... } catch { /* filesystem is auxiliary */ }` which silently swallowed the error, leaving state.json/events.ndjson/summary.md never written. All 6 failures stem from this — 2 read state.json directly (ENOENT), 1 relies on validation logic that reads state.json, 1 relies on listLiveStates scanning state.json files, and the others count derivative state that depends on the file existing.
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.evidence_pattern: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open 'C:\Users\...\aioson-live-command-*\.aioson\runtime\live\direct-session:1778803...:deyvin\state.json'
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.resolution: Added sessionKeyToDirName() helper in src/commands/live.js that replaces `:` with `__`. Applied in resolveLivePaths (single boundary point — all FS writes go through it). Exported the helper so tests can construct paths matching the on-disk layout. The session_key stored in SQLite / returned by CLI keeps its colons (it's a public identifier). live-command 8/8 + live-json-output 2/2 + runtime-command relevant test green. Full suite: 2413/2422 (was 2407/2422).
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.footnote: Feature was silently broken on Windows since commit 4621cf3 (feat: add tracked live sessions and runtime projections) — CI runs on Linux where colons are valid filenames so it never surfaced. Dashboard live view never worked on Windows before this fix.
files: tests/live-command.test.js:36:live session commands track start, plan progress, handoff and close for a no-launch session
tests/live-command.test.js:394:live:list returns sessions after start and close
tests/live-command.test.js:435:state.json contains events_by_type breakdown after emit
tests/live-json-output.test.js:34:live session commands return structured JSON payloads with plan progress and handoff
tests/runtime-command.test.js:610:runtime status exposes live sessions, micro-tasks, and handoffs from the live runtime flow
real-assertion-failure-live-cluster.suspected_source: Recent refactor in src/commands/live-* or src/live-session — verify state.json write path matches what live:start advertises

test-needs-update-new-check-noise.description: Tests stubbed dossierTelemetry.emitDossierEvent and counted calls. They were written before the active-learning-loop Phase 6 added `learning_loop_template_parity_violation`, which fired in both test scenarios because the synthetic tmpdir didn't replicate the new template artifacts.
files: tests/sync-agents-preflight.test.js:194:FIXED (refactored — filter by type + drop the global exit-code assertion since main() now reflects multiple parity checks)
test-needs-update-new-check-noise.resolution: Test 146 keeps its assertions but filters `calls` to entries with payload.type === 'sync_agents_parity_violation' before counting. Test 194 was renamed and rescoped: the original 'main() returns 0' assertion bundled two independent invariants (no parity_violation emit, AND global exit code 0). The exit code now depends on multiple parity checks (agent-chain + template + future additions), so the test focuses on the negative invariant that's actually agent-chain-specific: 'no sync_agents_parity_violation emit when agent-chain parity is clean'. sync-agents-preflight 7/7 green.

stale-fixture-path.description: Tests referenced docs/en/schemas/index.json but the directory had been moved via `git mv` to docs/en/5-reference/schemas/ in commit 20ac2fa (2026-05-07, 'docs(en): phase 1 — mirror docs/pt 5-layer structure'). The test wasn't updated alongside the move. Schemas themselves are present (22 files including index.json) — just at a new path.
stale-fixture-path.resolution: Single-line change: SCHEMAS_DIR constant repointed to docs/en/5-reference/schemas. Comment added referencing the git-mv commit so the next path drift is easier to trace. json-schema-files 2/2 green. Full suite: 2420/2422.

drift-from-recent-refactor.description: agent-contracts kernel size budget: BOTH product (18356 bytes) AND dev (18198 bytes) had grown past the 15000-byte budget — test only reported product because short-circuit on first failure. sheldon was inside (14481).
drift-from-recent-refactor.resolution: @architect decision: rebudget from 15000 → 20000 globally. Rationale: the on-demand-doc pattern (kernels reference external docs rather than inlining them) is still intact, so the cap protects against accidental bloat, not documented scope growth. Growth came from deliberate additions (active-learning-loop wiring, sub-task scout, dossier protocol, etc.). Next dev hitting 20KB will get the same nudge. agent-contracts test green.

real-assertion-failure-singleton.status: FIXED 2026-05-14 (@dev) — 2 real singletons + 1 absorbed by bug-005
real-assertion-failure-singleton.description: Two of the three singletons share a common Windows path-separator root cause: production code uses path.join which emits backslash on Windows; tests asserted forward-slash substrings. The third singleton (live-command.test.js:308) was misclassified — it was part of the live-cluster ENOENT chain and got fixed by bug-005.
files: tests/live-command.test.js:308:FIXED via bug-005 (not a singleton; was symptomatic of state.json never written)
real-assertion-failure-singleton.resolution: Both real singletons fixed by normalizing path output to forward-slash after path.join in user-facing fields (.replace(/\\\\/g, '/')). Decision: normalize at production boundary (not in tests) because these fields are emitted in JSON outputs, CLI logs, and consumed by downstream automation across platforms. Mixed separators would be an interop problem. agent-teams-adapter 36/36 + learning-auto-promote 10/10 green. Full suite: 2414/2422.

perf-flake-windows-io.description: QA-PERF-01 — p99 latency stays under 100ms across 500 sequential context:load calls. ROOT CAUSE refined: the test PASSES in isolation (~12s total for 500 calls, p99 under 100ms) but fails under full-suite parallel execution on NTFS — ~100 test files competing for SQLite/disk/temp IO push p99 to 1000-1300ms. Not Windows-slow-by-itself; Windows-slow-under-contention.
perf-flake-windows-io.resolution: @architect decision: skip on Windows only, via Node test-runner `skip` option with explanatory string. Initial attempt was platform-aware SLA (Win=250ms) but ambient contention pushes p99 to 1222ms — any cap that survives contention is too loose to detect real regressions. Skipping preserves signal where it matters (Linux/CI run strict 100ms bound) and stops the false-positive noise on Windows. To measure Windows perf manually: run `node --test tests/qa-telemetry-foundation.test.js` in isolation. Full suite now: 2421 pass / 0 fail / 1 skipped.
